competitions.product: WIN A NEO-VINTAGE ROLEX EXPLORER II "POLAR" 16570 W/ BOX & PAPERS SET
competitions.product_info: Home – Rolex Explorer 16570 “Polar” WIN A NEO-VINTAGE ROLEX EXPLORER II "POLAR" 16570 W/ BOX & PAPERS SET Value: £7,000 Duration: 7 Days Age: Dated 2001 Max. Entries: 699 Ticket Price: £20 Live Draw: 07/07/26 The Rolex Explorer II is one of the great modern classic wristwatches , and this is an excellent example of the 16570 reference . Dating to 2001 , and known as the “Polar” for its attractive white dial, this example is in excellent, sharp condition. The watch comes with its original box, papers, booklets, tag and more, and was originally sold in Hong Kong. 20.89% SOLD 0 Tickets Sold: 146 699 20.89% 553 Tickets remaining 553/699 Tickets Remaining Time Left to End: 5 Days 17 Hours 55 Minutes 53 Seconds ENTER NOW WIN A ROLEX EXPLORER II "POLAR" 16570 BOX & PAPERS ENTER NOW 20.89% SOLD 0 Tickets Sold: 146 699 20.89% 553 Tickets remaining 553/699 Tickets Remaining Time Left to End: 5 Days 17 Hours 55 Minutes 53 Seconds Value: £7,000 Duration: 7 Days Age: Dated 2001 Entries: 699 Max. Ticket Price: £20 Live Draw: 07/07/2026 The Rolex Explorer II is one of the great modern classic wristwatches , and this is an excellent example of the 16570 reference . Dating to 2001 , and known as the “Polar” for its attractive white dial, this example is in excellent, sharp condition. The watch comes with its original box, papers, booklets, tag and more, and was originally sold in Hong Kong. #31 - The Rolex Explorer II "Polar" 16570 w/ Original Box & Papers Set 2001 The History— The Example The Rolex Explorer II evolved from the earlier Explorer models by introducing a fixed 24-hour bezel and additional GMT hand, transforming it from a simple time-only tool watch into a purpose-built companion for explorers working in environments where distinguishing day from night could prove difficult. The reference 16570 is widely regarded as the sweet spot of the Explorer II lineage, combining classic proportions with the reliable Rolex calibre 3185 movement and independently adjustable GMT hand. Dating to 2001 , this “Polar” example features the model’s iconic white dial, offering superb legibility and a distinctive look that has become increasingly popular with collectors. Presented in excellent, sharp condition , the watch has been exceptionally well preserved and is accompanied by its original box, papers, booklets, tag and accessories . Originally supplied in Hong Kong , it is a superb, complete example of one of Rolex’s most wearable and increasingly collectible sports watches. After the initial idea over five years ago, we’re so excited to now bring this new concept to you. Curated by A Trusted Expert Our competitions are curated by Alex Stevens, a trusted & reputable vintage watch specialist with lifelong experience in the trade. 12 Month Service Warranty Every watch is fully serviced by our watchmaker & comes with a 12 month mechanical warranty, so you can enjoy your watch hassle-free. Collector-Grade Condition Every watch is meticulously curated by Alex, known for selecting only the finest, collector-grade examples in terms of condition & originality. Low & Transparent Odds We don’t flood the draw with tickets. Our competitions are deliberately limited to keep odds low & your chance of winning fair. Authenticity Guarantee Every watch is thoroughly inspected & fully authenticated by our in-house expert. We are professionals, not enthusiasts or hobbyists. Guaranteed Draws Every draw ends on the stated date, no matter what. We never extend our competitions, even if we haven't sold all the tickets. competitions.product: Win A Tudor Monarch
competitions.product_info: Chrono Win Competitions How it works Winners 0 Collectors Watch Competition Win This Tudor Monarch Time Left to End: 0 Days 21 Hrs 8 Mins 56 Secs 26.17% SOLD 443/600 Tickets Remaining Enter Now winners Year: 2026 Box and papers: Original box and papers included Collectors Watch Competition - Guaranteed Winners - Transparent Draws End Date: July 12, 2026 10:00 pm - Draw Date: 12/07/2026 10:30PM Collectors Watch Competition Win a Tudor Monarch TUDOR Monarch ref. M2639W1A0U-0001 — a brand-new 2026 release and one of TUDOR’s most interesting modern collector pieces. This example features the distinctive dark champagne-colour dial with applied Roman and Arabic hour markers, a finely faceted 39mm stainless steel case, polished and satin finishes, sapphire crystal, 100m water resistance and a two-link faceted steel bracelet with TUDOR’s excellent “T-fit” clasp. From a collector’s perspective, the Monarch is significant because it is not simply another Black Bay variation. It marks TUDOR revisiting one of its historic lines with a much more refined, design-led execution. The papyrus-toned dial, sharp case geometry and unusual numeral layout give it a very different character within the modern TUDOR catalogue. Inside is the Manufacture Calibre MT5662-2U, a self-winding mechanical movement with COSC and METAS certification, a 65-hour power reserve and high-grade finishing visible through the display case back. This gives the Monarch proper modern technical credibility while still carrying a strong vintage-inspired identity. Year: 2026 Condition: Brand new / unworn Box and papers: Original box and papers included A rare opportunity to enter for a first-year TUDOR Monarch — a watch that feels far more collector-focused than a standard daily sports model, combining TUDOR’s centenary design language with modern manufacture movement quality. competitions.product_info: Home – Cartier Paris Tank Louis 78086 WIN A VINTAGE CARTIER PARIS TANK LOUIS 78086 18K SOLID GOLD Value: £7,000 Duration: 7 Days Age: Circa 1980 Max. Ticket Price: £20 Live Draw: 14/07/2026 The Cartier Tank Louis is one of history’s most iconic wristwatches, adored by so many distinguished individuals of the last century. This is one of its best iterations – the 78086 reference in 18k solid gold , with the sought-after Paris-signed dial. A very chic, elegant wristwatch for both men and women. This example is in excellent, unpolished condition with a pristine “Mk 3” dial . #32 - The Cartier Paris Tank Louis 78086 18k Solid Gold The History— The Example The Cartier Tank Louis is one of the defining wristwatch designs of the last century. First introduced in 1922 as a refinement of the original Tank, the Tank Louis established the elegant proportions that have come to define Cartier, and has been worn by artists, royalty and cultural icons ever since. This reference, the 78086 , is one of the most desirable vintage iterations. Crafted in 18k solid yellow gold , it features the highly sought-after Paris-signed dial , a detail that collectors have long admired for its rarity and historical significance. This particular watch is presented in excellent, unpolished condition , retaining crisp case lines and a pristine Mk 3 dial . The beautifully balanced proportions make it equally elegant on both men and women, wearing with the understated sophistication that has made the Tank Louis such an enduring classic. A refined and increasingly collectible Cartier – timeless in design, beautifully preserved, and one of the finest expressions of the Tank Louis lineage. competitions.product: Win This ROLEX Datejust 36 ref. 16233
competitions.product_info: Chrono Win Competitions How it works Winners 0 Collectors Watch Competition Win This ROLEX Datejust 36 ref. 16233 Time Left to End: 4 Days 20 Hrs 7 Mins 11 Secs 1.33% SOLD 592/600 Tickets Remaining Enter Now winners Value: £6500 The Rolex Datejust 36 ref. 16233 is a circa-1990s example with a 36mm stainless-steel case, 18ct yellow-gold fluted bezel and steel-and-gold Jubilee bracelet. It features a striking blue vignette diamond dial, sapphire crystal and quickset date with Cyclops magnifier, powered by Rolex’s automatic calibre 3135. The watch is supplied with its original box and papers and combines classic five-digit Datejust proportions with one of the most distinctive dial configurations from the era. Collectors Watch Competition - Guaranteed Winners - Transparent Draws End Date: July 19, 2026 10:00 pm - Draw Date: 19/07/2026 10:30PM Collectors Watch Competition Win a ROLEX Datejust 36 ref. 16233 ROLEX Datejust 36 ref. 16233 — a standout two-tone Datejust from one of ROLEX’s most recognisable and wearable eras. This example features the highly desirable blue vignette dial with factory diamond hour markers, a 36mm stainless steel Oyster case, 18ct yellow gold fluted bezel, sapphire crystal, Cyclops date window and the classic steel and yellow gold Jubilee bracelet. From a collector’s perspective, the dial is what sets this watch apart. Standard champagne, silver and white Datejust 16233 examples are widely seen, but the blue vignette diamond dial gives this piece a much more distinctive personality. The gradient effect, fading darker around the edge of the dial, brings serious 1990s ROLEX character and gives the watch far more presence than a standard two-tone Datejust. The 16233 also sits in an important sweet spot for collectors. It retains the classic 36mm Datejust proportions and traditional two-tone luxury aesthetic, while benefiting from modern usability features such as sapphire crystal, quickset date and the highly regarded ROLEX calibre 3135 automatic movement. This is exactly the type of Datejust collectors increasingly look for: recognisable, wearable, liquid, and with a dial configuration that makes it stand out from the ordinary examples on the market. Reference: 16233 Case size: 36mm Case: Stainless steel and 18ct yellow gold Bezel: 18ct yellow gold fluted bezel Bracelet: Steel and yellow gold Jubilee bracelet Dial: Blue vignette diamond dial Movement: ROLEX calibre 3135 automatic Crystal: Sapphire crystal Condition: Excellent Box and papers: Included A rare opportunity to enter for a characterful ROLEX Datejust 36 with one of the most eye-catching dial configurations of the five-digit era — a proper collector’s Datejust with classic two-tone ROLEX appeal. Ticket Price: £25 Live Draw: 21/07/2026 The 16610LV “Kermit” is one of its most collectible modern iterations of one of the most iconic watches ever. Introduced in 2003 to celebrate the Submariner’s 50th anniversary, it features its distinctive green bezel insert and “Maxi” dial . This example dates to 2007 and is in excellent condition , accompanied by its original box, guarantee card, Rolex service card , and bookets. Overall, a superb example of an increasingly sought-after modern classic . #33 - The Rolex Submariner Kermit 16610LV w/ Box, Papers & Service Card 2007 The History— The Example The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic and recognisable wristwatches ever made, and the reference 16610LV “Kermit” has become one of the most collectible modern iterations of the model. Introduced in 2003 to celebrate the 50th anniversary of the Submariner, the Kermit marked a notable departure from the classic all-black configuration. Its distinctive green aluminium bezel insert was paired with a larger “Maxi” dial and broader luminous hour markers, giving the watch a character of its own while retaining the classic proportions of the 16610. Powered by the Rolex calibre 3135 automatic movement, the 16610LV offers the durability and everyday practicality for which the Submariner is renowned. The aluminium bezel, slimmer case profile and classic Oyster bracelet give it a distinctly different feel to the ceramic Submariners that followed, and have helped establish the Kermit as a firm collector favourite. This example dates to 2007 and is presented in excellent condition. It is accompanied by its original box and guarantee card, alongside a Rolex service card – making it a strong example of this increasingly sought-after reference. A distinctive modern classic and an important chapter in the Submariner’s history – highly wearable, instantly recognisable and increasingly collectible. competitions.product: Win This ROLEX Datejust 36 ref. 16233
competitions.product_info: Chrono Win Competitions How it works Winners 0 Collectors Watch Competition Win This ROLEX Datejust 36 ref. 16233 Time Left to End: 4 Days 20 Hrs 7 Mins 11 Secs 1.33% SOLD 592/600 Tickets Remaining Enter Now winners Value: £6500 The Rolex Datejust 36 ref. 16233 is a circa-1990s example with a 36mm stainless-steel case, 18ct yellow-gold fluted bezel and steel-and-gold Jubilee bracelet. It features a striking blue vignette diamond dial, sapphire crystal and quickset date with Cyclops magnifier, powered by Rolex’s automatic calibre 3135. The watch is supplied with its original box and papers and combines classic five-digit Datejust proportions with one of the most distinctive dial configurations from the era. Collectors Watch Competition - Guaranteed Winners - Transparent Draws End Date: July 19, 2026 10:00 pm - Draw Date: 19/07/2026 10:30PM Collectors Watch Competition Win a ROLEX Datejust 36 ref. 16233 ROLEX Datejust 36 ref. 16233 — a standout two-tone Datejust from one of ROLEX’s most recognisable and wearable eras. This example features the highly desirable blue vignette dial with factory diamond hour markers, a 36mm stainless steel Oyster case, 18ct yellow gold fluted bezel, sapphire crystal, Cyclops date window and the classic steel and yellow gold Jubilee bracelet. From a collector’s perspective, the dial is what sets this watch apart. Standard champagne, silver and white Datejust 16233 examples are widely seen, but the blue vignette diamond dial gives this piece a much more distinctive personality. The gradient effect, fading darker around the edge of the dial, brings serious 1990s ROLEX character and gives the watch far more presence than a standard two-tone Datejust. The 16233 also sits in an important sweet spot for collectors. It retains the classic 36mm Datejust proportions and traditional two-tone luxury aesthetic, while benefiting from modern usability features such as sapphire crystal, quickset date and the highly regarded ROLEX calibre 3135 automatic movement. This is exactly the type of Datejust collectors increasingly look for: recognisable, wearable, liquid, and with a dial configuration that makes it stand out from the ordinary examples on the market. Reference: 16233 Case size: 36mm Case: Stainless steel and 18ct yellow gold Bezel: 18ct yellow gold fluted bezel Bracelet: Steel and yellow gold Jubilee bracelet Dial: Blue vignette diamond dial Movement: ROLEX calibre 3135 automatic Crystal: Sapphire crystal Condition: Excellent Box and papers: Included A rare opportunity to enter for a characterful ROLEX Datejust 36 with one of the most eye-catching dial configurations of the five-digit era — a proper collector’s Datejust with classic two-tone ROLEX appeal. Ticket Price: £25 Live Draw: 21/07/2026 The 16610LV “Kermit” is one of its most collectible modern iterations of one of the most iconic watches ever. Introduced in 2003 to celebrate the Submariner’s 50th anniversary, it features its distinctive green bezel insert and “Maxi” dial . This example dates to 2007 and is in excellent condition , accompanied by its original box, guarantee card, Rolex service card , and bookets. Overall, a superb example of an increasingly sought-after modern classic . #33 - The Rolex Submariner Kermit 16610LV w/ Box, Papers & Service Card 2007 The History— The Example The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic and recognisable wristwatches ever made, and the reference 16610LV “Kermit” has become one of the most collectible modern iterations of the model. Introduced in 2003 to celebrate the 50th anniversary of the Submariner, the Kermit marked a notable departure from the classic all-black configuration. Its distinctive green aluminium bezel insert was paired with a larger “Maxi” dial and broader luminous hour markers, giving the watch a character of its own while retaining the classic proportions of the 16610. Powered by the Rolex calibre 3135 automatic movement, the 16610LV offers the durability and everyday practicality for which the Submariner is renowned. The aluminium bezel, slimmer case profile and classic Oyster bracelet give it a distinctly different feel to the ceramic Submariners that followed, and have helped establish the Kermit as a firm collector favourite. This example dates to 2007 and is presented in excellent condition. It is accompanied by its original box and guarantee card, alongside a Rolex service card – making it a strong example of this increasingly sought-after reference. A distinctive modern classic and an important chapter in the Submariner’s history – highly wearable, instantly recognisable and increasingly collectible.
